title: Week 6 Review
html:
Congratulations on completing week six! After finishing all the lessons you should now know:

  • 9 new kanji
  • 12 new words in katakana
  • How to express the result of an action, express instruction/request, to express a sequence in actions/directions and to express if you have (ever) done something/have experienced doing something
  • Use of に (frequency), the form て+もいいです (is it ok to/may I?), the form て+はいけません  (must do/need to do),   何 + classifier + も　(a number of…)
  • Counting occurrences of an action nankai, ikkai (何回、一回）
  • How to use 5 new grammar points:
    • Grammar focus on the expressions maitoshi, maitsuki, yoku (毎年、毎月、よく)
    • Grammar focus on もう一ど, すぐ (time), どうぞ、どうも (greeting)
    • Grammar focus on the use of どうやって、すぐ、さきに、もうすこし
    • Grammar focus on と to specify the partner in an action
If you have any questions on what you have learned this week, don’t forget that you can always ask for more help in the student community!
